column11: Multiple questions on python and sql

column9:
1)Candidates were selected based on resume shortlisting, for Data Engg. intern role the resume should have related projects along with python libraries like pandas, numpy etc. and sql.
 2) T1- Discussion about resume and internships, python theoretical questions, sql theoretical questions, codes like palindrome, hash-maps or dictionaries, python data structures, queries like second highest salary, common table expressions etc.
 3) T2- Interview was taken by Lead ML engineer, no python and sql questions asked, discussion started with ML and projects, topics like collinearity, curse of dimensionality, one hot encoding, undersampling and oversampling, ML models, linear regression equations and best fit had to be judges based on a graph drawn by the interviewer, epsilon or bias meaning in wx+b, what is slope and some basic like pi, tan45,e value, logistic regression, threshold value of LR, ROC curve, Bell curve, Standard normal distribution, Confusion matrix, accuracy, sensitivity and specificity, Conditional probability and Bayes theorem, Lots of other statistics concepts that I never heard before, -------overall the style of interview depends on the interviewer, be thorough with your basics and every word on your resume
 4) HR- Virtual - Discussion about company, my goals, how internship will benefit me, biggest life lesson from college apart from academics and general questions.
